title: robinia pseudoacacia subsp. inermis
intro: Robinia pseudoacacia subsp. inermis, also known as the thornless black locust, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to North America. The plant contains rotenone, a natural compound with medicinal properties, which has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The thornless black locust has been shown to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the thornless black locust can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
